zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Margaret Harleston於1370年出生在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾時，她的父親Roger Harleston42歲，母親Lady Margaret de Walton30歲。她於大約1389年與John De Burgoyne Esq.在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1397年在她的家鄉享年28歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Margaret Harleston于1370年出生在英国英格兰坎布利其爾时，她的父亲Roger Harleston42岁，母亲Lady Margaret de Walton30岁。她于大约1389年与John De Burgoyne Esq.在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1397年在她的家乡享年28岁。

nameMeanings.name: Harleston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (West Lothian) and English: patronymic, with intrusive -t-, from the personal name Charles (compare Charleson ). The various places called Charleston are all of recent origin, so they are unlikely to be the source of the surname.

variants.name: Charles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Antoine, Andre, Francois, Patrice, Germaine, Magalie, Philippe, Yves, Alain, Jacques, Cecile.

French, Welsh, English, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Tanzania): from the French form of the ancient Germanic personal name Karl ‘man, husband, freeman’, which was Latinized as Carolus. In France the personal name was popular from an early date, due to the fame of the Emperor Charlemagne (c. 742–814; Latin name Carolus Magnus, i.e. Charles the Great). The Old French form Charles was briefly introduced to England by the Normans, but was rare during the main period of surname formation. It was introduced more successfully to Scotland in the 16th century by the Stuarts, who had strong ties with France, and was brought by them to England in the 17th century. Its frequency as a Welsh surname is attributable to the late date of Welsh surname formation. Old English Ceorl ‘peasant’ is also found as a byname, but the resulting Middle English form, Charl, with a patronymic in -s, if it existed at all, would have been absorbed by the French form introduced by the Normans. English variants pronounced with initial k- for the most part reflect the cognate Old Norse personal name Karl, Karli. Compare Carl .

English: in some cases, possibly a habitational name from Charles (Devon), or a post-medieval variant of Cherrill, with excrescent -s, perhaps by false association with the personal name Charles. Cherrill is either a habitational name from Cherhill in Wilshire, or a status name from Middle English cherl, cheril, charl, chirl ‘churl, bonded tenant, serf; peasant’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Margaret Harleston was born in 1370, in Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Roger Harleston, was 42 and her mother, Lady Margaret de Walton, was 30. She married John De Burgoyne Esq. about 1389, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1397, in her hometown, at the age of 28.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Margaret Harleston est née en 1370, à Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger Harleston, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret de Walton, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé John De Burgoyne Esq. environ 1389, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1397, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 28 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice Margaret Harleston 1370 in Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger Harleston 42 und ihre Mutter Lady Margaret de Walton 30. Sie hat um 1389 in England, United Kingdom John De Burgoyne Esq. geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1397 im Alter von 28 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Margaret Harleston è nata il nel 1370 nel Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Roger Harleston, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Lady Margaret de Walton, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato John De Burgoyne Esq. circa 1389, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1397, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 28 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Margaret Harlestonが1370年にイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアで生まれた時，父Roger Harlestonは42歳，母Lady Margaret de Waltonは30歳であった。約1389年にイギリスイギリスでJohn De Burgoyne Esq.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1397年に故郷で28歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Margaret Harleston(이)가 1370년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Harleston(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret de Walton(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 대략 1389년 영국 잉글랜드에서 John De Burgoyne Esq.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1397년 그녀의 고향에서 28세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Margaret Harleston nasceu em 1370, em Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger Harleston, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret de Walton, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com John De Burgoyne Esq. aproximadamente 1389, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1397, em sua cidade natal, com 28 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Margaret Harleston родилась в 1370 г. в местности камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Roger Harleston было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret de Walton, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John De Burgoyne Esq. около 1389 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1397 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 28 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice Margaret Harleston nació en 1370, en Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Roger Harleston, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Lady Margaret de Walton, tenía 30 años. Se casó con John De Burgoyne Esq. aproximadamente 1389, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1397, en su pueblo, a la edad de 28 años.
